ead.archdesc:
======= RMM02736Henry Wright papers2736Compiled by H. FinchDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20022B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
planThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:30:31 UTC.ENG12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Henry Wright papers
Wright, Henry, -1936.
2736
.9 cubic feet. .9 cubic feet.
1907-1960,-1907-1938 (bulk) Project reports, article notes, and biographical material. Includes project descriptions and reports on garden cities, housing research; the relation of city planning to architecture; article notes written jointly with Lewis Mumford; and biographical material. Collection material in English 2068830
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
H. Finch
Date completed:
February 1973
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, Ann Hubert, May - July 2002
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, August 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Henry Wright was a city planner.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes project descriptions and reports on garden cities, some of which are with Clarence Stein; housing research, some on war housing and some done with Albert Mayer; the relation of city planning to architecture; article notes written jointly with Lewis Mumford; and biographical material.
Cite As:
Henry Wright Papers, #2736. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
City planners. Housing -- Effect of wars on. Housing. City planning. Architecture. Mayer, Albert, 1897-1981 Mumford, Lewis, 1895-1990. Stein, Clarence S.
2068830 <<<<<<< HEAD Folders 1-30Biographical data prepared by HWAlbert Mayer, "Henry Wright: Creative Planner" Survey Graphic,September 19361Henry Churchill, "Henry Wright: 1876-1936" Journal of AIPNovember 19601Benjamin M. Gruzen, "Henry Wright,...A List of References"19361A City Plan for Saint Louis (HW was a member of one of the committees)19071"Planning Country Home Grounds and Gardens," 4 p. pamphlet19091No Man's Land of Real Estate..., 7 p. manuscript19191"Planning City Areas for Small Homes," Supplement to The Journal of the American Institute of Architects, 16 p.August 19201Untitled manuscript, 4 p.,19201"Shall We Community Plan?," The Journal of the American Institute of Architects, 5 p.October 19211Correspondence: To, Lewis Mumford, August 17 with clipping; From, Nebraska Masonic Home, October 819231"Housing Progress," The Journal of the American Institute of Architects, 2 p. with L.M. [Lewis Mumford?]December 19231Modern Homes for City Dwellers, 23 p. brochure [mutilated]19231Garden Cities Studies. A group of 18 studies, drafts and notes relative to the development of garden cities. All listed here because of their subject unity.August 1923-19261What is to be Learned from Housing Developments of the Past in America, 11 p. typescriptAugust 13, 19231Real Estate Development, 3 p. typescript,August 13, 19231War Housing in the United States, 2 drafts, 7 p. typescript,August 13, 19231Critical Examination of the Relation of Site Use and Cost to the Housing Problem in America, 15 p. typescriptAugust 13, 19231Addenda, 28 p. typescript,August 13, 19231Further Investigation in the Field of Site Development Costs in Relation to Housing, 5 p. typescript, 6 sheets graphicsAugust 24, 19231Garden City Studies, 7 p. typescriptAugust 28, 19231Garden City Studies, 4 p. typescript,September 3, 19231A Critical Examination Of Present Zoning Practice in Relation to the Housing Situation, 2 p. typescriptSeptember 3, 19231[Drafts of Five Sections], 8 p. typescript, "For Mumford,"September 11, 19231Housing Developments in the Past, 2 drafts, one signed by Lewis Mumford, 16 p. typescript19231Garden City Reports, Superseded, 40 p. typescript, 16 sheets of graphics19231Garden City 1st Report, Not Final, 27 p. typescript19231Garden City Next to Last Report, Not Final, 41 p. typescript; subtitle: Preliminary Study of a Proposed Garden Community in the New York Region by Alexander M. Bing, Henry Wright, Clarence S. Stein. [p. 20 missing]19231Notes, 12 p.19231Garden Cities, 19 p. manuscriptJuly 25-28, 19241Garden Cities, 15 p. typescript,August 5, 19241Garden City Research, 9 p. typescript, E. Standish Palmer,January-February 19261First Annual Report of the Stockholders of Associated Specialists, Inc., (HW was First Vice-President)19241Report of D.M. Kendall and Henry Wright on their Investigation of the Present Speculative Building Operations now in Progress in Brooklyn and Long Island, 22p. typescript, 19 sheets of graphics and photographsMay 19241Folders 31-49"Exhibit: Preliminary Studies of Natural Features and Economic Development of the State," Commission of Housing and Regional Planning, 8 p. brochure,April 20-25, 19252"The Road to Good Houses," Survey Graphic, 5 p. issue on planningMay 19252The Home Ideal Versus Reality, 4 p. typescript,December 15, 19252"The Home Ideal Versus Reality," American Federationist, 5 p.January 19262"Housing: How Much for How Much?," The Survey Midmonthly, 5 p.March 15, 19262Memorandum in regard to proposed regional planning report, 1 p. typescript,March 16, 19262An Account of Sunnyside Gardens in Relation to Problems and Development of Design and Site Planning..., 21 p. typescriptAugust 16, 19262Sunnyside Gardens an Experiment in Planning, 10 p. typescript19262Study of Application of Sunnyside Planning Principles to a Larger City Area, 23 p. typescript and graphics19262[C.A. Beard], "Economy, Charm and Safety of the Cul-de-Sac Street," The American City, 4 p. [The author, identified as an "eminent English town planner," comments on Radburn.]Nov. 19282Untitled typescript, 17 p., annotated "AIA C.P.C."19282"Group Housing," Building Developer, 8 p.August 19292"How to Reduce the Cost of Land Development," National Real Estate Journal, 6 p.September 2, 19292Some Principles Relating to the Economics of Land Subdivision," American City Planning Institute, 12 p. Preprint No. 1Nov. 19292Some Principles Relating to the Economics of Land Subdivision, Paper No. 1. 20 p. pamphlet including the article above and comment on it by members of the Institute19302Housing Research Completed under the Direction of Henry Wright, 84 p. typescript and 11 sheets of graphicsJanuary-April 19302Housing Research, Division II Only, The Problems and Relative Advantages of Smaller Types of Dwellings..., 56 p. typescript and 10 pages of graphics,January-April 19302"Picturesque Architecture," The Architecture Forum, 7 p.February 19312"The Place of the Apartment in the Modern Community," The Architectural Record, 31 p., plus 6 p. multilith,March 19302"The Autobiography of Another Idea," reprint from The Western Architect, 7 p.September 19302Folders 50-92Discussion of Neighborhood Planning..., 6 p. typescriptJanuary 17, 19313"The Architect, the Plan and the City," The Architectural Forum, 7 p.February 19313"The Apartment House: A Review and Forecast," The Architectural Record, 16 p.,March 19313The President's Conference on Home Building and Home Ownership. Tentative Report of the Committee on Subdivision Layout. 82 p. pamphlet, [HW was a member of the committee]December 3, 19313Proceedings: Committee on Design of the President's Conference Home Building and Home Ownership, 33 p. typescriptDecember 3, 19313Summary of Further Developments of "A Housing Research" for the Consideration of the Temporary Housing Commission of Illinois, 81 p. typescript19313The In Betweens of Community Planning, Student Memo: Lecture Series, 3 p. multilithFebruary 15, 19323Will a New Dwelling Type Solve the Problem of Providing Low Rentals for the Lower-Income Groups?, 4 p. multilithMarch 12, 19323"The Cost of Housing," The Architectural Forum, 6 p.March 19323Memorandum, To Clarence S. Stein, 3 p.April 7, 19323"A Self - Starter for the Building Industry," Building Investment, 6 p.June 19323"Hillside Group Housing," The Architectural Record, 14 p.,October 19323Hillside Housing Development, Memorandum to Clarence S. Stein, 7 p.October 29, 19323Summary and Further Development...Temporary Housing Commission of Illinois, 82 p. typescript, [See item in 1931]19323"Are We Ready for an American Housing Advance?," Architecture, 8 p.June 19333"Housing-Where, When, and How?," Architecture, 32 p., forward by Lewis MumfordJuly 19333"Housing - Where, When, and How?," Part Two, 31 p.August 19333"Sinking Slums," Survey Graphic, 3 p.August 19333Notes to Lewis Mumford re: New Republican Articles. 8 p. typescriptJanuary 22, 19343"New Homes for a New Deal," 46 p. pamphlet, with Albert Mayer and Lewis MumfordFebruary 14, 19343"New Homes for a New Deal, Part II Abolishing Slums Forever," 4 p. article from The New Republic, Feb. 21, 1934. "Part IV A Concrete Program," 4 p.March 7, 1934Feb. 21, 19343Questions Relating to Slum Clearance and Low Cost Housing Problems, 9 p. typescriptAugust 31, 19343"Housing Conditions in Relation to Scientific Machine Production," The Journal of the Franklin Institute, 13 p. reprint,October 19343Memorandum with respect to report of recent tour of European housing experts... 2 p. typescript19343Article No. 1, 10 p. typescript, relates to New Deal housing19343Article No. 2, 11 p. typescript, relates to New Deal housing19343Oscar Fisher, "Buckingham: Housing Laboratory," The Architectural Record, 18 p. reprint. "One of Henry Wright's last designs...," 2 other items Re: Buckingham19383To Plan or Not to Plan, 7 p. typescript3Article re: problem of subdivisions, 5 p. typescript3Some Lesson on the Land Sub-Division Problem, 6 p. typescript3Confidential to Housing board Advising Committee, 13 p. typescript3The Public Interest in Good Housing, 7 p. typescript3Town Planning in the United States, 5 p. typescript3The Fallacy of High Buildings: A Swing of the Pendulum, 8 p. typescript3The Architect and Prosperity, 6 p. typescript3Land Subdivision, 17 p. typescript3Queens-Astoria: A Study in Realistic City Planning, 3 p. typescript with Carol Aronovici, Henry S. Churchill, William Lescraze, Albert Mayer3Wanted a Subdividion for the Gridiron Street System, 3 p. typescript3Too Much Planning or Too Many Plans? 5 p. typescript fragment3Outline of a book, 15 p. typescript3A.I.A.
